TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.FORM: izua i Makazayazaya aza ti Tjukutjuku putsekeɫ tjai Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu, puaɫak tjai Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan.
S.PHON: izua i makazajazaja aza ti cukucuku puʦəkəʎ cai kuʎəʎuʎəʎu, puaʎak cai puʎaʎuʎaʎujan.
S.FORM: izua i Makazayazaya aza ti Tjukutjuku pucekelj tjai Kuljeljuljelju, pualjak tjai Puljaljuljaljuyan.
S.PHON: izua i makazajazaja aza ti ʦukuʦuku puʦəkəʎ ʦai kuʎəʎuʎəʎu, puaʎak ʦai puʎaʎuʎaʎujan.
S.TRANSL: In Makazayazaya there was a person called Tjukutjuku who married Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu, and they had a child called Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan.

S.FORM: i Makalaulauz ti Muakakai putsekeɫ tjai Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan, puaɫak tjai Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: i makaɭauɭauz ti muakakai puʦəkəʎ cai puʎaʎuʎaʎujan, puaʎak cai cukucuku.
S.FORM: i Makalaulauz ti Muakakai pucekelj tjai Puljaljuljaljuyan, pualjak tjai Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: i makaɭauɭauz ti muakakai puʦəkəʎ ʦai puʎaʎuʎaʎujan, puaʎak ʦai ʦukuʦuku.
S.TRANSL: In Makalaulauz Muakakai married Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan, and their child was Tjukutjuku.

S.FORM: vaik ti Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan a kisudju tjai Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: vaik ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan a kisuɉu cai cukucuku.
S.FORM: vaik ti Puljaljuljaljuyan a kisudju tjai Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: vaik ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan a kisuɉu ʦai ʦukuʦuku.
S.TRANSL: Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan went to court Tjukutjuku.

S.FORM: sipakatjeluɫ vaik a tsemikel sema Makazayazaya.
S.PHON: sipakacəɭuʎ vaik a ʦəmikəɭ səma makazajazaja.
S.FORM: sipakatjelulj vaik a cemikel sema Makazayazaya.
S.PHON: sipakaʦəɭuʎ vaik a ʦəmikəɭ səma makazajazaja.
S.TRANSL: On the third day he went back to Makazayazaya.

S.FORM: "sa-u, Vungavan, kivadaqi tu kemudakuda a silisi."
S.PHON: "sa-u, vuŋavan, kivadaqi tu kəmudakuda a siɭisi."
S.FORM: "sa-u, Vungavan, kivadaqi tu kemudakuda a silisi."
S.PHON: "sa-u, vuŋavan, kivadaqi tu kəmudakuda a siɭisi."
S.TRANSL: “Vungavan, go and ask about the bride-price.”

S.FORM: "aku a izua za putjaumaumaɫ aza lisian tu mamazangiɫan?
S.PHON: "aku a izua za pucaumaumaʎ aza ɭisian tu mamazaŋiʎanʔ
S.FORM: "aku a izua za putjaumaumalj aza lisian tu mamazangiljan?
S.PHON: "aku a izua za puʦaumaumaʎ aza ɭisian tu mamazaŋiʎan?
S.TRANSL: “Why should there be any change in the bride-price for chiefs?

S.FORM: taiday za taiday anga; pusiɫevavaw tu tapuluq."
S.PHON: taidaj za taidaj aŋa; pusiʎəvavaw tu tapuɭuq."
S.FORM: taiday za taiday anga; pusiljevavaw tu tapuluq."
S.PHON: taidaj za taidaj aŋa; pusiʎəvavaw tu tapuɭuq."
S.TRANSL: A hundred is a hundred, and add ten.”

S.FORM: "anema aya, vuvu i Vungavan?"
S.PHON: "anəma aja, vuvu i vuŋavanʔ"
S.FORM: "anema aya, vuvu i Vungavan?"
S.PHON: "anəma aja, vuvu i vuŋavan?"
S.TRANSL: “What do they say, Granny Vungavan?”

S.FORM: "'aku a izua za putjaumaumaɫ aza lisian tu mamazangiɫan?
S.PHON: "'aku a izua za pucaumaumaʎ aza ɭisian tu mamazaŋiʎanʔ
S.FORM: "'aku a izua za putjaumaumalj aza lisian tu mamazangiljan?
S.PHON: "ʔaku a izua za puʦaumaumaʎ aza ɭisian tu mamazaŋiʎan?
S.TRANSL: “They just say: ‘Why should there be any change in bride-price for chiefs?

S.FORM: taiday za taiday anga; pusiɫevavaw tu tapuluq', aya sakamaya."
S.PHON: taidaj za taidaj aŋa; pusiʎəvavaw tu tapuɭuq', aja sakamaja."
S.FORM: taiday za taiday anga; pusiljevavaw tu tapuluq', aya sakamaya."
S.PHON: taidaj za taidaj aŋa; pusiʎəvavaw tu tapuɭuqʔ, aja sakamaja."
S.TRANSL: A hundred is a hundred, and add ten.’”

S.FORM: "ki tjen a qemaɫup nutiaw, ɫa qalian, a kiveɫangaw tja sipaputsekeɫ.
S.PHON: "ki cən a qəmaʎup nutiaw, ʎa qaɭian, a kivəʎaŋaw ca sipapuʦəkəʎ.
S.FORM: "ki tjen a qemaljup nutiaw, lja qalian, a kiveljangaw tja sipapucekelj.
S.PHON: "ki ʦən a qəmaʎup nutiaw, ʎa qaɭian, a kivəʎaŋaw ʦa sipapuʦəkəʎ.
S.TRANSL: “We’ll go hunting tomorrow, friends, and get head-wreaths for the wedding.

S.FORM: ari qaɫupi.
S.PHON: ari qaʎupi.
S.FORM: ari qaljupi.
S.PHON: ari qaʎupi.
S.TRANSL: Come on, let’s go hunting.

S.FORM: kiveɫangaɫangavu a maqatsuvutsuvung.
S.PHON: kivəʎaŋaʎaŋavu a maqaʦuvuʦuvuŋ.
S.FORM: kiveljangaljangavu a maqacuvucuvung.
S.PHON: kivəʎaŋaʎaŋavu a maqaʦuvuʦuvuŋ.
S.TRANSL: Young people, collect the head-wreaths;

S.FORM: vetsiku a ramaɫemaɫeng.
S.PHON: vəʦiku a ramaʎəmaʎəŋ.
S.FORM: veciku a ramaljemaljeng.
S.PHON: vəʦiku a ramaʎəmaʎəŋ.
S.TRANSL: old people carve the meat.

S.FORM: tja su tjatjainu anan a ɫavalian?"
S.PHON: ca su cacainu anan a ʎavaɭianʔ"
S.FORM: tja su tjatjainu anan a ljavalian?"
S.PHON: ʦa su ʦaʦainu anan a ʎavaɭian?"
S.TRANSL: How should the grass decorations be?”

S.FORM: "tja su tjakakeḍi anan."
S.PHON: "ca su cakakəɖi anan."
S.FORM: "tja su tjakakedri anan."
S.PHON: "ʦa su ʦakakəɖi anan."
S.TRANSL: “The smaller the better.”

S.FORM: "ari saumaqi anga."
S.PHON: "ari saumaqi aŋa."
S.FORM: "ari saumaqi anga."
S.PHON: "ari saumaqi aŋa."
S.TRANSL: “Come on, let’s go home.”

S.FORM: "tjumaqu anan a kaiv."
S.PHON: "cumaqu anan a kaiv."
S.FORM: "tjumaqu anan a kaiv."
S.PHON: "ʦumaqu anan a kaiv."
S.TRANSL: “Go home and have supper.”

S.FORM: tsikel anga a maqatsuvutsuvung a ramaɫemaɫeng, sa seman veɫangaɫangaw a maqatsuvutsuvung, seman vutjuvutjuɫ a ramaɫemaɫeng.
S.PHON: ʦikəɭ aŋa a maqaʦuvuʦuvuŋ a ramaʎəmaʎəŋ, sa səman vəʎaŋaʎaŋaw a maqaʦuvuʦuvuŋ, səman vucuvucuʎ a ramaʎəmaʎəŋ.
S.FORM: cikel anga a maqacuvucuvung a ramaljemaljeng, sa seman veljangaljangaw a maqacuvucuvung, seman vutjuvutjulj a ramaljemaljeng.
S.PHON: ʦikəɭ aŋa a maqaʦuvuʦuvuŋ a ramaʎəmaʎəŋ, sa səman vəʎaŋaʎaŋaw a maqaʦuvuʦuvuŋ, səman vuʦuvuʦuʎ a ramaʎəmaʎəŋ.
S.TRANSL: The young and old go back, and the young make head-wreaths, and the old deal with the meat.

S.FORM: "ari anga a paputsekeɫ a sema Makalaulauz."
S.PHON: "ari aŋa a papuʦəkəʎ a səma makaɭauɭauz."
S.FORM: "ari anga a papucekelj a sema Makalaulauz."
S.PHON: "ari aŋa a papuʦəkəʎ a səma makaɭauɭauz."
S.TRANSL: “Let’s go to Makalaulauz for the wedding.”

S.FORM: sa vaik a tamaqaɫan tjaɫu sasukuzan.
S.PHON: sa vaik a tamaqaʎan caʎu sasukuzan.
S.FORM: sa vaik a tamaqaljan tjalju sasukuzan.
S.PHON: sa vaik a tamaqaʎan ʦaʎu sasukuzan.
S.TRANSL: So the whole village went to the resting place.

S.FORM: matu matateztez sinikuang.
S.PHON: matu matatəztəz sinikuaŋ.
S.FORM: matu matateztez sinikuang.
S.PHON: matu matatəztəz sinikuaŋ.
S.TRANSL: Shots echoed round. (?)

S.FORM: "tsasavu, ɫa Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: "ʦasavu, ʎa cukucuku.
S.FORM: "casavu, lja Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: "ʦasavu, ʎa ʦukuʦuku.
S.TRANSL: “Come out, Tjukutjuku clan.

S.FORM: uzay anga a se Makazayazaya."
S.PHON: uzaj aŋa a sə makazajazaja."
S.FORM: uzay anga a se Makazayazaya."
S.PHON: uzaj aŋa a sə makazajazaja."
S.TRANSL: Makazayazaya people are here.”

S.FORM: "uzay anga nia apu.
S.PHON: "uzaj aŋa nia apu.
S.FORM: "uzay anga nia apu.
S.PHON: "uzaj aŋa nia apu.
S.TRANSL: “Here’s our betel-nut.

S.FORM: ki mun a namaqusaw anga, ki mun a namavali anga, nu sika i djalan anga."
S.PHON: ki mun a namaqusaw aŋa, ki mun a namavaɭi aŋa, nu sika i ɉaɭan aŋa."
S.FORM: ki mun a namaqusaw anga, ki mun a namavali anga, nu sika i djalan anga."
S.PHON: ki mun a namaqusaw aŋa, ki mun a namavaɭi aŋa, nu sika i ɉaɭan aŋa."
S.TRANSL: You will be thirsty and wind-blown from the journey.”

S.FORM: "ui anga.
S.PHON: "ui aŋa.
S.FORM: "ui anga.
S.PHON: "ui aŋa.
S.TRANSL: “Yes indeed,

S.FORM: namaqusaw anga men, namavali anga men, nia sika i djalan anga."
S.PHON: namaqusaw aŋa mən, namavaɭi aŋa mən, nia sika i ɉaɭan aŋa."
S.FORM: namaqusaw anga men, namavali anga men, nia sika i djalan anga."
S.PHON: namaqusaw aŋa mən, namavaɭi aŋa mən, nia sika i ɉaɭan aŋa."
S.TRANSL: we are thirsty and wind-blown from our journey.

S.FORM: sipakatjeluɫ "ari anga.
S.PHON: sipakacəɭuʎ "ari aŋa.
S.FORM: sipakatjelulj "ari anga.
S.PHON: sipakaʦəɭuʎ "ari aŋa.
S.TRANSL: On the third day they said: “Let’s go now.

S.FORM: mavurvur anga ɫa tja kakeḍian ɫa tja ramaɫemaɫeng."
S.PHON: mavurvur aŋa ʎa ca kakəɖian ʎa ca ramaʎəmaʎəŋ."
S.FORM: mavurvur anga lja tja kakedrian lja tja ramaljemaljeng."
S.PHON: mavurvur aŋa ʎa ʦa kakəɖian ʎa ʦa ramaʎəmaʎəŋ."
S.TRANSL: Our children and old people will be anxious.”

S.FORM: ta tsaviɫ anga ti Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan i Makalaulauz; qau sa ika puaɫak.
S.PHON: ta ʦaviʎ aŋa ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan i makaɭauɭauz; qau sa ika puaʎak.
S.FORM: ta cavilj anga ti Puljaljuljaljuyan i Makalaulauz; qau sa ika pualjak.
S.PHON: ta ʦaviʎ aŋa ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan i makaɭauɭauz; qau sa ika puaʎak.
S.TRANSL: Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan was a year in Makalaulauz; but no child was born.

S.FORM: "sa-u, Vungavan.
S.PHON: "sa-u, vuŋavan.
S.FORM: "sa-u, Vungavan.
S.PHON: "sa-u, vuŋavan.
S.TRANSL: “Go, Vungavan,

S.FORM: 'idu alapan amen ta vaqu i salang,' aya-u ti Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan."
S.PHON: 'idu aɭapan amən ta vaqu i saɭaŋ,' aja-u ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan."
S.FORM: 'idu alapan amen ta vaqu i salang,' aya-u ti Puljaljuljaljuyan."
S.PHON: ʔidu aɭapan amən ta vaqu i saɭaŋ,ʔ aja-u ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan."
S.TRANSL: tell Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan: ‘Get us some millet from the granary.’”

S.FORM: "aku a mangtjez sun, vuvu i Vungavan?"
S.PHON: "aku a maŋcəz sun, vuvu i vuŋavanʔ"
S.FORM: "aku a mangtjez sun, vuvu i Vungavan?"
S.PHON: "aku a maŋʦəz sun, vuvu i vuŋavan?"
S.TRANSL: “Why have you come, Granny Vungavan?”

S.FORM: "singelit aken tjanumun."
S.PHON: "siŋəɭit akən canumun."
S.FORM: "singelit aken tjanumun."
S.PHON: "siŋəɭit akən ʦanumun."
S.TRANSL: “I missed you.”

S.FORM: "anema aya ti Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan?"
S.PHON: "anəma aja ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujanʔ"
S.FORM: "anema aya ti Puljaljuljaljuyan?"
S.PHON: "anəma aja ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan?"
S.TRANSL: “What does Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan say?”

S.FORM: "marekutj aken a patjanema.
S.PHON: "marəkuc akən a pacanəma.
S.FORM: "marekutj aken a patjanema.
S.PHON: "marəkuʦ akən a paʦanəma.
S.TRANSL: “I was afraid to say anything

S.FORM: nu ku patsunan a namare kikavetsengel."
S.PHON: nu ku paʦunan a namarə kikavəʦəŋəɭ."
S.FORM: nu ku pacunan a namare kikavecengel."
S.PHON: nu ku paʦunan a namarə kikavəʦəŋəɭ."
S.TRANSL: when I saw all the crops.” (?)

S.FORM: "sa-u, Vurivuri.
S.PHON: "sa-u, vurivuri.
S.FORM: "sa-u, Vurivuri.
S.PHON: "sa-u, vurivuri.
S.TRANSL: “Go, Vurivuri,

S.FORM: qaqivu ti Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan."
S.PHON: qaqivu ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan."
S.FORM: qaqivu ti Puljaljuljaljuyan."
S.PHON: qaqivu ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan."
S.TRANSL: speak to Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan.”

S.FORM: "aku a mangtjeztjez sun, vuvu i Vurivuri."
S.PHON: "aku a maŋcəzcəz sun, vuvu i vurivuri."
S.FORM: "aku a mangtjeztjez sun, vuvu i Vurivuri."
S.PHON: "aku a maŋʦəzʦəz sun, vuvu i vurivuri."
S.TRANSL: “Why have you come, Granny Vurivuri?”

S.FORM: "'idu,' aya tia tjaina, 'alapan amen ta vaqu a sema salang'."
S.PHON: "'idu,' aja tia caina, 'aɭapan amən ta vaqu a səma saɭaŋ'."
S.FORM: "'idu,' aya tia tjaina, 'alapan amen ta vaqu a sema salang'."
S.PHON: "ʔidu,ʔ aja tia ʦaina, ʔaɭapan amən ta vaqu a səma saɭaŋʔ."
S.TRANSL: “Your mother’s family say: ‘Get some millet for us from the granary.’”

S.FORM: "aku a sikaɫaɫava tjanuaken?"
S.PHON: "aku a sikaʎaʎava canuakənʔ"
S.FORM: "aku a sikaljaljava tjanuaken?"
S.PHON: "aku a sikaʎaʎava ʦanuakən?"
S.TRANSL: “Why are you waiting for me?”

S.FORM: "ui, ari, anema aya?
S.PHON: "ui, ari, anəma ajaʔ
S.FORM: "ui, ari, anema aya?
S.PHON: "ui, ari, anəma aja?
S.TRANSL: “Yes, come on. What does he say?

S.FORM: nakuya tsu ika mun a puaɫak.
S.PHON: nakuja ʦu ika mun a puaʎak.
S.FORM: nakuya cu ika mun a pualjak.
S.PHON: nakuja ʦu ika mun a puaʎak.
S.TRANSL: It’s bad that you have no child.

S.FORM: kitjuvadayu sakamaya."
S.PHON: kicuvadaju sakamaja."
S.FORM: kitjuvadayu sakamaya."
S.PHON: kiʦuvadaju sakamaja."
S.TRANSL: Get on and separate.”

S.FORM: "iya, manu anema nu siaya?
S.PHON: "ija, manu anəma nu siajaʔ
S.FORM: "iya, manu anema nu siaya?
S.PHON: "ija, manu anəma nu siaja?
S.TRANSL: “Oh, so what are you saying?

S.FORM: iru aken a pukaui a kitjuvaday."
S.PHON: iru akən a pukaui a kicuvadaj."
S.FORM: iru aken a pukaui a kitjuvaday."
S.PHON: iru akən a pukaui a kiʦuvadaj."
S.TRANSL: I won’t agree to separate.”

S.FORM: "anema aya tia kama tia kina tjanusun?"
S.PHON: "anəma aja tia kama tia kina canusunʔ"
S.FORM: "anema aya tia kama tia kina tjanusun?"
S.PHON: "anəma aja tia kama tia kina ʦanusun?"
S.TRANSL: “What do your father and mother say to you?”

S.FORM: "inika anema aya."
S.PHON: "inika anəma aja."
S.FORM: "inika anema aya."
S.PHON: "inika anəma aja."
S.TRANSL: “Nothing.”

S.FORM: sa qemauqaung tia Tjukutjuku kati Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan.
S.PHON: sa qəmauqauŋ tia cukucuku kati puʎaʎuʎaʎujan.
S.FORM: sa qemauqaung tia Tjukutjuku kati Puljaljuljaljuyan.
S.PHON: sa qəmauqauŋ tia ʦukuʦuku kati puʎaʎuʎaʎujan.
S.TRANSL: And Tjukutjuku and Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan cried.

S.FORM: lemigu a se Kanavanavaɫ tu mavurvurvur tia Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: ɭəmigu a sə kanavanavaʎ tu mavurvurvur tia cukucuku.
S.FORM: lemigu a se Kanavanavalj tu mavurvurvur tia Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: ɭəmigu a sə kanavanavaʎ tu mavurvurvur tia ʦukuʦuku.
S.TRANSL: The Kanavanavaɫ people heard that the Tjukutjuku family were anxious.

S.FORM: "ari, kisudju-i a sema Makalaulauz," aya.
S.PHON: "ari, kisuɉu-i a səma makaɭauɭauz," aja.
S.FORM: "ari, kisudju-i a sema Makalaulauz," aya.
S.PHON: "ari, kisuɉu-i a səma makaɭauɭauz," aja.
S.TRANSL: “Come on, let’s go courting to Makalaulauz,” they said.

S.FORM: saka izuanan ti Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan.
S.PHON: saka izuanan ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan.
S.FORM: saka izuanan ti Puljaljuljaljuyan.
S.PHON: saka izuanan ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan.
S.TRANSL: Now Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan was still there.

S.FORM: "uzay anga a se Kanavanavaɫ a muri kisudju tjanusun.
S.PHON: "uzaj aŋa a sə kanavanavaʎ a muri kisuɉu canusun.
S.FORM: "uzay anga a se Kanavanavalj a muri kisudju tjanusun.
S.PHON: "uzaj aŋa a sə kanavanavaʎ a muri kisuɉu ʦanusun.
S.TRANSL: “There are Kanavanavaɫ people here who want to court you.

S.FORM: ɫakua iru a sika suḍam uta.
S.PHON: ʎakua iru a sika suɖam uta.
S.FORM: ljakua iru a sika sudram uta.
S.PHON: ʎakua iru a sika suɖam uta.
S.TRANSL: But it’s not to be resented.

S.FORM: imazamazanan aken.
S.PHON: imazamazanan akən.
S.FORM: imazamazanan aken.
S.PHON: imazamazanan akən.
S.TRANSL: I’m still here.

S.FORM: qau a marekakakaka itjen a puraketj.
S.PHON: qau a marəkakakaka icən a purakəc.
S.FORM: qau a marekakakaka itjen a puraketj.
S.PHON: qau a marəkakakaka iʦən a purakəʦ.
S.TRANSL: We are siblings for ever.

S.FORM: tsasavu, ɫa Tjukutjuku," aya ti Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan.
S.PHON: ʦasavu, ʎa cukucuku," aja ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan.
S.FORM: casavu, lja Tjukutjuku," aya ti Puljaljuljaljuyan.
S.PHON: ʦasavu, ʎa ʦukuʦuku," aja ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan.
S.TRANSL: Come out, Tjukutjuku clan,” said Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan.

S.FORM: "iru aken a pukaui."
S.PHON: "iru akən a pukaui."
S.FORM: "iru aken a pukaui."
S.PHON: "iru akən a pukaui."
S.TRANSL: “I won’t agree.”

S.FORM: "kana ika sun a pukaui, ki navaik aken a sema Makazayazaya."
S.PHON: "kana ika sun a pukaui, ki navaik akən a səma makazajazaja."
S.FORM: "kana ika sun a pukaui, ki navaik aken a sema Makazayazaya."
S.PHON: "kana ika sun a pukaui, ki navaik akən a səma makazajazaja."
S.TRANSL: “If you don’t agree, I’ll go off to Makazayazaya.”

S.FORM: sa vaik anga ti Tjukutjuku a sema tsasaw.
S.PHON: sa vaik aŋa ti cukucuku a səma ʦasaw.
S.FORM: sa vaik anga ti Tjukutjuku a sema casaw.
S.PHON: sa vaik aŋa ti ʦukuʦuku a səma ʦasaw.
S.TRANSL: So Tjukutjuku went outside.

S.FORM: "uzay anga nia apu kinarasingan.
S.PHON: "uzaj aŋa nia apu kinarasiŋan.
S.FORM: "uzay anga nia apu kinarasingan.
S.PHON: "uzaj aŋa nia apu kinarasiŋan.
S.TRANSL: “Here’s our betel-nut and beer.

S.FORM: tekelu anga.
S.PHON: təkəɭu aŋa.
S.FORM: tekelu anga.
S.PHON: təkəɭu aŋa.
S.TRANSL: Have a drink.

S.FORM: ki mun a namaqusaw anga; ki mun a namavali anga; nu sika i djalan anga, nu sika i sasukuzan anga."
S.PHON: ki mun a namaqusaw aŋa; ki mun a namavaɭi aŋa; nu sika i ɉaɭan aŋa, nu sika i sasukuzan aŋa."
S.FORM: ki mun a namaqusaw anga; ki mun a namavali anga; nu sika i djalan anga, nu sika i sasukuzan anga."
S.PHON: ki mun a namaqusaw aŋa; ki mun a namavaɭi aŋa; nu sika i ɉaɭan aŋa, nu sika i sasukuzan aŋa."
S.TRANSL: You’ll be thirsty and wind-blown from your journey and from being at the resting place.”

S.FORM: "ui, nia gaugavi, sa nia apu-i, sa nia tekeli.
S.PHON: "ui, nia gaugavi, sa nia apu-i, sa nia təkəɭi.
S.FORM: "ui, nia gaugavi, sa nia apu-i, sa nia tekeli.
S.PHON: "ui, nia gaugavi, sa nia apu-i, sa nia təkəɭi.
S.TRANSL: “Yes, we accept; we’ll chew betel and drink.

S.FORM: namaqusaw anga men, namavali anga men a paquliqulid."
S.PHON: namaqusaw aŋa mən, namavaɭi aŋa mən a paquɭiquɭid."
S.FORM: namaqusaw anga men, namavali anga men a paquliqulid."
S.PHON: namaqusaw aŋa mən, namavaɭi aŋa mən a paquɭiquɭid."
S.TRANSL: We certainly are thirsty and wind-blown.”

S.FORM: sipakatjeluɫ "ari anga."
S.PHON: sipakacəɭuʎ "ari aŋa."
S.FORM: sipakatjelulj "ari anga."
S.PHON: sipakaʦəɭuʎ "ari aŋa."
S.TRANSL: On the third day they said: “Now let’s go.”

S.FORM: sa sema Kanavanavaɫ anga tjaɫu sasukuzan.
S.PHON: sa səma kanavanavaʎ aŋa caʎu sasukuzan.
S.FORM: sa sema Kanavanavalj anga tjalju sasukuzan.
S.PHON: sa səma kanavanavaʎ aŋa ʦaʎu sasukuzan.
S.TRANSL: And they went to Kanavanavaɫ to the resting place.

S.FORM: "sa-u, vuvu i Vungavan, tsikelu sa kivadaqi tu kemudakuda a silisi."
S.PHON: "sa-u, vuvu i vuŋavan, ʦikəɭu sa kivadaqi tu kəmudakuda a siɭisi."
S.FORM: "sa-u, vuvu i Vungavan, cikelu sa kivadaqi tu kemudakuda a silisi."
S.PHON: "sa-u, vuvu i vuŋavan, ʦikəɭu sa kivadaqi tu kəmudakuda a siɭisi."
S.TRANSL: “Go, Granny Vungavan; go back and ask the bride-price.”

S.FORM: "aku a izua putjaumaumaɫ aza lisian tu mamazangiɫan?
S.PHON: "aku a izua pucaumaumaʎ aza ɭisian tu mamazaŋiʎanʔ
S.FORM: "aku a izua putjaumaumalj aza lisian tu mamazangiljan?
S.PHON: "aku a izua puʦaumaumaʎ aza ɭisian tu mamazaŋiʎan?
S.TRANSL: “Why should there be any change in the bride-price for chiefs?

S.FORM: taiday za taiday anga; pusiɫevavaw tu tapuluq."
S.PHON: taidaj za taidaj aŋa; pusiʎəvavaw tu tapuɭuq."
S.FORM: taiday za taiday anga; pusiljevavaw tu tapuluq."
S.PHON: taidaj za taidaj aŋa; pusiʎəvavaw tu tapuɭuq."
S.TRANSL: A hundred is a hundred; and add ten.”

S.FORM: "anema aya?"
S.PHON: "anəma ajaʔ"
S.FORM: "anema aya?"
S.PHON: "anəma aja?"
S.TRANSL: “What do they say?”

S.FORM: "'aku a izua za putjaumaumaɫ aza lisian tu mamazangiɫan?
S.PHON: "'aku a izua za pucaumaumaʎ aza ɭisian tu mamazaŋiʎanʔ
S.FORM: "'aku a izua za putjaumaumalj aza lisian tu mamazangiljan?
S.PHON: "ʔaku a izua za puʦaumaumaʎ aza ɭisian tu mamazaŋiʎan?
S.TRANSL: “They just say: ‘Why should there be any change in the bride-price for chiefs?

S.FORM: taiday za taiday anga; pusiɫevavaw tu tapuluq,' aya sakamaya."
S.PHON: taidaj za taidaj aŋa; pusiʎəvavaw tu tapuɭuq,' aja sakamaja."
S.FORM: taiday za taiday anga; pusiljevavaw tu tapuluq,' aya sakamaya."
S.PHON: taidaj za taidaj aŋa; pusiʎəvavaw tu tapuɭuq,ʔ aja sakamaja."
S.TRANSL: A hundred is a hundred; and add ten.’”

S.FORM: "ari, anema aya?
S.PHON: "ari, anəma ajaʔ
S.FORM: "ari, anema aya?
S.PHON: "ari, anəma aja?
S.TRANSL: “Come on, what do they say?

S.FORM: qaɫupi, sa tja kiveɫangavi."
S.PHON: qaʎupi, sa ca kivəʎaŋavi."
S.FORM: qaljupi, sa tja kiveljangavi."
S.PHON: qaʎupi, sa ʦa kivəʎaŋavi."
S.TRANSL: Let’s go hunting, and collect head-wreaths.”

S.FORM: sipakapusaɫ a qemaɫuqaɫup.
S.PHON: sipakapusaʎ a qəmaʎuqaʎup.
S.FORM: sipakapusalj a qemaljuqaljup.
S.PHON: sipakapusaʎ a qəmaʎuqaʎup.
S.TRANSL: They hunted for two days.

S.FORM: "kiveɫangavu a maqatsuvutsuvung, vutsiqu a ramaɫemaɫeng.
S.PHON: "kivəʎaŋavu a maqaʦuvuʦuvuŋ, vuʦiqu a ramaʎəmaʎəŋ.
S.FORM: "kiveljangavu a maqacuvucuvung, vuciqu a ramaljemaljeng.
S.PHON: "kivəʎaŋavu a maqaʦuvuʦuvuŋ, vuʦiqu a ramaʎəmaʎəŋ.
S.TRANSL: “Young people, collect the head-wreaths; old people carve the meat.

S.FORM: tja su tjatjainu anan a ɫavalian?"
S.PHON: ca su cacainu anan a ʎavaɭianʔ"
S.FORM: tja su tjatjainu anan a ljavalian?"
S.PHON: ʦa su ʦaʦainu anan a ʎavaɭian?"
S.TRANSL: How should the grass decorations be?”

S.FORM: "tja su tjakakeḍi anan.
S.PHON: "ca su cakakəɖi anan.
S.FORM: "tja su tjakakedri anan.
S.PHON: "ʦa su ʦakakəɖi anan.
S.TRANSL: “The smaller the better.”

S.FORM: tjezua aza.
S.PHON: cəzua aza.
S.FORM: tjezua aza.
S.PHON: ʦəzua aza.
S.TRANSL: “That’s enough.

S.FORM: ari saumaqi.
S.PHON: ari saumaqi.
S.FORM: ari saumaqi.
S.PHON: ari saumaqi.
S.TRANSL: Come on, let’s go home.

S.FORM: maselem a qadaw.
S.PHON: masəɭəm a qadaw.
S.FORM: maselem a qadaw.
S.PHON: masəɭəm a qadaw.
S.TRANSL: Daylight is going.

S.FORM: surava-i a tja kiniveɫangaw a tja inalap.
S.PHON: surava-i a ca kinivəʎaŋaw a ca inaɭap.
S.FORM: surava-i a tja kiniveljangaw a tja inalap.
S.PHON: surava-i a ʦa kinivəʎaŋaw a ʦa inaɭap.
S.TRANSL: Let’s unload the head-wreaths we’ve collected.

S.FORM: sa-u kaivu anan."
S.PHON: sa-u kaivu anan."
S.FORM: sa-u kaivu anan."
S.PHON: sa-u kaivu anan."
S.TRANSL: Go and get supper.”

S.FORM: tsemikel anga a maqatsuvutsuvung a ramaɫemaɫeng.
S.PHON: ʦəmikəɭ aŋa a maqaʦuvuʦuvuŋ a ramaʎəmaʎəŋ.
S.FORM: cemikel anga a maqacuvucuvung a ramaljemaljeng.
S.PHON: ʦəmikəɭ aŋa a maqaʦuvuʦuvuŋ a ramaʎəmaʎəŋ.
S.TRANSL: The young and old come back.

S.FORM: "sanu veɫangaɫangavu a maqatsuvutsuvung, sanu vutjuɫu a ramaɫemaɫeng.
S.PHON: "sanu vəʎaŋaʎaŋavu a maqaʦuvuʦuvuŋ, sanu vucuʎu a ramaʎəmaʎəŋ.
S.FORM: "sanu veljangaljangavu a maqacuvucuvung, sanu vutjulju a ramaljemaljeng.
S.PHON: "sanu vəʎaŋaʎaŋavu a maqaʦuvuʦuvuŋ, sanu vuʦuʎu a ramaʎəmaʎəŋ.
S.TRANSL: “Young people, make wreaths; old people, deal with the meat.

S.FORM: ki tjen a vaik a nutiaw a sema Makalaulauz a putsekeɫ.
S.PHON: ki cən a vaik a nutiaw a səma makaɭauɭauz a puʦəkəʎ.
S.FORM: ki tjen a vaik a nutiaw a sema Makalaulauz a pucekelj.
S.PHON: ki ʦən a vaik a nutiaw a səma makaɭauɭauz a puʦəkəʎ.
S.TRANSL: Tomorrow we’ll go to Makalaulauz for the wedding.

S.FORM: ari a tamaqaɫan."
S.PHON: ari a tamaqaʎan."
S.FORM: ari a tamaqaljan."
S.PHON: ari a tamaqaʎan."
S.TRANSL: Let the whole village go.”

S.FORM: uzay anga a se Kanavanavaɫ i sasukuzan anga.
S.PHON: uzaj aŋa a sə kanavanavaʎ i sasukuzan aŋa.
S.FORM: uzay anga a se Kanavanavalj i sasukuzan anga.
S.PHON: uzaj aŋa a sə kanavanavaʎ i sasukuzan aŋa.
S.TRANSL: The Kanavanavaɫ people were there at the resting place.

S.FORM: matu mateztez a sinikuang.
S.PHON: matu matəztəz a sinikuaŋ.
S.FORM: matu mateztez a sinikuang.
S.PHON: matu matəztəz a sinikuaŋ.
S.TRANSL: Shots echoed round.

S.FORM: namaya i Makalaulauz a uta; sa pangtjez i katsasavan.
S.PHON: namaja i makaɭauɭauz a uta; sa paŋcəz i kaʦasavan.
S.FORM: namaya i Makalaulauz a uta; sa pangtjez i kacasavan.
S.PHON: namaja i makaɭauɭauz a uta; sa paŋʦəz i kaʦasavan.
S.TRANSL: The same in Makalaulauz too. So they reached the courtyard.

S.FORM: "tsasavu, ɫa Tjukutjuku."
S.PHON: "ʦasavu, ʎa cukucuku."
S.FORM: "casavu, lja Tjukutjuku."
S.PHON: "ʦasavu, ʎa ʦukuʦuku."
S.TRANSL: “Come out, Tjukutjuku clan.”

S.FORM: "iru aken a pukaui."
S.PHON: "iru akən a pukaui."
S.FORM: "iru aken a pukaui."
S.PHON: "iru akən a pukaui."
S.TRANSL: “I won’t agree.”

S.FORM: qemaung sakamaya ti Tjukutjuku; namaya ti Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan.
S.PHON: qəmauŋ sakamaja ti cukucuku; namaja ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan.
S.FORM: qemaung sakamaya ti Tjukutjuku; namaya ti Puljaljuljaljuyan.
S.PHON: qəmauŋ sakamaja ti ʦukuʦuku; namaja ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan.
S.TRANSL: Tjukutjuku just cries. So does Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan.

S.FORM: "tsasavu sakamaya.
S.PHON: "ʦasavu sakamaja.
S.FORM: "casavu sakamaya.
S.PHON: "ʦasavu sakamaja.
S.TRANSL: “Just come out.

S.FORM: kana ika sun a pukaui, ki navaik aken a sema Makazayazaya."
S.PHON: kana ika sun a pukaui, ki navaik akən a səma makazajazaja."
S.FORM: kana ika sun a pukaui, ki navaik aken a sema Makazayazaya."
S.PHON: kana ika sun a pukaui, ki navaik akən a səma makazajazaja."
S.TRANSL: If you don’t agree, I’ll go to Makazayazaya.”

S.FORM: sa tsasaw anga ti Tjukutjuku sipakatjeluɫ.
S.PHON: sa ʦasaw aŋa ti cukucuku sipakacəɭuʎ.
S.FORM: sa casaw anga ti Tjukutjuku sipakatjelulj.
S.PHON: sa ʦasaw aŋa ti ʦukuʦuku sipakaʦəɭuʎ.
S.TRANSL: So Tjukutjuku came out on the third day.

S.FORM: sa vaik ti Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan a sema Makazayazaya.
S.PHON: sa vaik ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan a səma makazajazaja.
S.FORM: sa vaik ti Puljaljuljaljuyan a sema Makazayazaya.
S.PHON: sa vaik ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan a səma makazajazaja.
S.TRANSL: And Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan went to Makazayazaya.

S.FORM: sipakasimuluq matsay ti Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan.
S.PHON: sipakasimuɭuq maʦaj ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan.
S.FORM: sipakasimuluq macay ti Puljaljuljaljuyan.
S.PHON: sipakasimuɭuq maʦaj ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan.
S.TRANSL: On the tenth day Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan died.

S.FORM: sipakivadaq tu pasainu a tsemveɫ.
S.PHON: sipakivadaq tu pasainu a ʦəmvəʎ.
S.FORM: sipakivadaq tu pasainu a cemvelj.
S.PHON: sipakivadaq tu pasainu a ʦəmvəʎ.
S.TRANSL: They asked where he should be buried.

S.FORM: "pasazua-u aken ta pautez na kadjunangan a pasa kanavanavaɫ."
S.PHON: "pasazua-u akən ta pautəz na kaɉunaŋan a pasa kanavanavaʎ."
S.FORM: "pasazua-u aken ta pautez na kadjunangan a pasa kanavanavalj."
S.PHON: "pasazua-u akən ta pautəz na kaɉunaŋan a pasa kanavanavaʎ."
S.TRANSL: “Put me at the edge of the land towards the south(-east) (or Kanavanavaɫ).”

S.FORM: matsay anga makeɫang ti Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan.
S.PHON: maʦaj aŋa makəʎaŋ ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan.
S.FORM: macay anga makeljang ti Puljaljuljaljuyan.
S.PHON: maʦaj aŋa makəʎaŋ ti puʎaʎuʎaʎujan.
S.TRANSL: Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan was known to be dead.

S.FORM: "ari kivangavangi a sema Kanavanavaɫ," aya ti Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: "ari kivaŋavaŋi a səma kanavanavaʎ," aja ti cukucuku.
S.FORM: "ari kivangavangi a sema Kanavanavalj," aya ti Tjukutjuku.
S.PHON: "ari kivaŋavaŋi a səma kanavanavaʎ," aja ti ʦukuʦuku.
S.TRANSL: “Let’s go to Kanavanavaɫ,” said Tjukutjuku.

S.FORM: tjaɫu zua ta tsineveɫan tjai Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan.
S.PHON: caʎu zua ta ʦinəvəʎan cai puʎaʎuʎaʎujan.
S.FORM: tjalju zua ta cineveljan tjai Puljaljuljaljuyan.
S.PHON: ʦaʎu zua ta ʦinəvəʎan ʦai puʎaʎuʎaʎujan.
S.TRANSL: They went to where Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan was buried.

S.FORM: "tjaula-i aken.
S.PHON: "cauɭa-i akən.
S.FORM: "tjaula-i aken.
S.PHON: "ʦauɭa-i akən.
S.TRANSL: “Leave me here.

S.FORM: kitsungal anan aken," aya, sa kisan kaḍiaḍi, sa vaik a minlayap tia Tjukutjuku kati Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan.
S.PHON: kiʦuŋaɭ anan akən," aja, sa kisan kaɖiaɖi, sa vaik a minɭajap tia cukucuku kati puʎaʎuʎaʎujan.
S.FORM: kicungal anan aken," aya, sa kisan kadriadri, sa vaik a minlayap tia Tjukutjuku kati Puljaljuljaljuyan.
S.PHON: kiʦuŋaɭ anan akən," aja, sa kisan kaɖiaɖi, sa vaik a minɭajap tia ʦukuʦuku kati puʎaʎuʎaʎujan.
S.TRANSL: I’m going to pay my respects,” she said, and turned into a butterfly. So Tjukutjuku and Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan flew away.

S.FORM: sa manu vaik anga kivadaq ta qadaw: "kemuda kana ika men a puaɫak?"
S.PHON: sa manu vaik aŋa kivadaq ta qadaw: "kəmuda kana ika mən a puaʎakʔ"
S.FORM: sa manu vaik anga kivadaq ta qadaw: "kemuda kana ika men a pualjak?"
S.PHON: sa manu vaik aŋa kivadaq ta qadaw: "kəmuda kana ika mən a puaʎak?"
S.TRANSL: And so they went to ask the sun: “Why can’t we have children?”

S.FORM: pavay tu tjara mau ita nia sialangan anga a qadaw, sa puaɫak tjai Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu.
S.PHON: pavaj tu cara mau ita nia siaɭaŋan aŋa a qadaw, sa puaʎak cai kuʎəʎuʎəʎu.
S.FORM: pavay tu tjara mau ita nia sialangan anga a qadaw, sa pualjak tjai Kuljeljuljelju.
S.PHON: pavaj tu ʦara mau ita nia siaɭaŋan aŋa a qadaw, sa puaʎak ʦai kuʎəʎuʎəʎu.
S.TRANSL: The sun gave each of them one source of life, and they had a child called Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu.

S.FORM: sema katsauan aza ti Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu; vaik a sema Makazayazaya, sa penaɫipaɫi a sinipapipuɫuan ta anemaneman anga.
S.PHON: səma kaʦauan aza ti kuʎəʎuʎəʎu; vaik a səma makazajazaja, sa pənaʎipaʎi a sinipapipuʎuan ta anəmanəman aŋa.
S.FORM: sema kacauan aza ti Kuljeljuljelju; vaik a sema Makazayazaya, sa penaljipalji a sinipapipuljuan ta anemaneman anga.
S.PHON: səma kaʦauan aza ti kuʎəʎuʎəʎu; vaik a səma makazajazaja, sa pənaʎipaʎi a sinipapipuʎuan ta anəmanəman aŋa.
S.TRANSL: This Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu came into the world, and went to Makazayazaya, and (magically) destroyed all the signs of mourning.

S.FORM: "tima tsu?
S.PHON: "tima ʦuʔ
S.FORM: "tima cu?
S.PHON: "tima ʦu?
S.TRANSL: “Who is this?

S.FORM: kemasinu tsu nu qaɫan?
S.PHON: kəmasinu ʦu nu qaʎanʔ
S.FORM: kemasinu cu nu qaljan?
S.PHON: kəmasinu ʦu nu qaʎan?
S.TRANSL: What village does he come from?

S.FORM: tja djameqaw sakamaya."
S.PHON: ca ɉaməqaw sakamaja."
S.FORM: tja djameqaw sakamaya."
S.PHON: ʦa ɉaməqaw sakamaja."
S.TRANSL: We’ll just kill him.”

S.FORM: "qadjaw nimun.
S.PHON: "qaɉaw nimun.
S.FORM: "qadjaw nimun.
S.PHON: "qaɉaw nimun.
S.TRANSL: “It’s up to you.

S.FORM: ɫa vuvu i Tjukutjuku, ɫa vuvu i Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu.
S.PHON: ʎa vuvu i cukucuku, ʎa vuvu i kuʎəʎuʎəʎu.
S.FORM: lja vuvu i Tjukutjuku, lja vuvu i Kuljeljuljelju.
S.PHON: ʎa vuvu i ʦukuʦuku, ʎa vuvu i kuʎəʎuʎəʎu.
S.TRANSL: Granny Tjukutjuku, Grandpa Kuɫeɫuɫeɫu,

S.FORM: timunay a sangasangasan a djemameq tjanuaken.
S.PHON: timunaj a saŋasaŋasan a ɉəmaməq canuakən.
S.FORM: timunay a sangasangasan a djemameq tjanuaken.
S.PHON: timunaj a saŋasaŋasan a ɉəmaməq ʦanuakən.
S.TRANSL: you should be the first to kill me.

S.FORM: aɫak aken nia kina i Tjukutjuku, nia kama i Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan."
S.PHON: aʎak akən nia kina i cukucuku, nia kama i puʎaʎuʎaʎujan."
S.FORM: aljak aken nia kina i Tjukutjuku, nia kama i Puljaljuljaljuyan."
S.PHON: aʎak akən nia kina i ʦukuʦuku, nia kama i puʎaʎuʎaʎujan."
S.TRANSL: Tjukutjuku was my mother; Puɫaɫuɫaɫuyan was my father.”

S.FORM: sa izuanga sakamaya.
S.PHON: sa izuaŋa sakamaja.
S.FORM: sa izuanga sakamaya.
S.PHON: sa izuaŋa sakamaja.
S.TRANSL: So he stayed on there.
